rulings.Case Title: 270 TURK STREET LLC VS. JULIO COUOH et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, January 2, 2015, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANTs 270 TURK STREET LLC, VERITAS INVESTMENTSK INC.-CASE# 541124, YAT-PANG AU, GREENTREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC.-CASE# 541124's DEMURRER Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the arguments of (1) res judicata (Pelletier v. Alameda Yacht Harbor (1986) 188 Cal.App.3d 1551l and (2) insufficiency of allegations re: tenancy and waiver (complaint sufficiently alleges both; landlords improperly raise multiple issues of fact on demurrer). Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the sham pleading argument. This Court's October 30, 2014 ordered Mena "to clarify the inconsistencies" instead Mena completely changed substantive allegations (See Complaint paragraph 26 v. FAC paragraph 22) without any explanation for such change. Argument in the opposition re: unspecified typographical error is insufficient to plead around prior inconsistent allegations. Mena shall have 10 days from the notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint specifically addressing the substance and reason for discrepancies in complaint and FAC. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: PEI JUNG DING VS. DAVID WILCOX
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, January 2, 2015, line 2. DEFENDANT DAVID WILCOX's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges unlawful detainer based on non-payment of rent. Non-compliance with SFRO is an affirmative defense that should be raised in the answer. Staudigl v. Harper (1946) 76 Cal.App.2d 439 and Wheeler v. Bainbrigde (1948) 84 Cal.App.2d Supp. 849 do not address local rent ordinances. = (501/RBU)
